schema.dart.purpose: Defines Drift database tables for offline-first architecture
tables: Trips - Complete trip data with sync tracking
Journals - Journal entries with foreign key to trips
SyncMetadata - Sync state monitoring per entity type
key_features: All tables include sync-specific fields (isSynced, hasPendingChanges, version)
Strategic indexes for common query patterns
Soft delete support with isDeleted flag
JSON fields for flexible data storage (travelCompanionIds, imageUrls, interests, preferences)

offline_database.dart.purpose: Central export hub for database infrastructure

patterns_discovered.database_design: Consistent offline-first pattern with sync tracking across all tables
patterns_discovered.sync_fields: Standardized sync fields: isSynced, hasPendingChanges, version, lastSyncedAt
patterns_discovered.soft_deletion: Universal soft delete pattern with isDeleted flag instead of hard deletes
patterns_discovered.json_storage: Use of JSON text columns for flexible data (arrays/objects)
patterns_discovered.indexing_strategy: Composite indexes for common query patterns and sync status filtering
patterns_discovered.foreign_key_relationships: Clear relationships between Trips->Journals and User ownership

gotchas_discovered: Schema complexity: 371 lines for a single schema file suggests high complexity
No error handling visible in schema definition
JSON storage may need custom serialization/deserialization
Conflict resolution only tracks version number, no detailed merge strategy
Soft delete cleanup not automated - requires manual cleanup process

approach_outcome.completeness: Complete database schema with all 5 required tables implemented
approach_outcome.quality: High - follows Drift ORM conventions with comprehensive documentation
approach_outcome.offline_first_ready: Schema supports offline-first with proper sync tracking
approach_outcome.implementation_notes: Created comprehensive schema with sync fields, indexes, and proper relationships. Ready for next phases (entity models, DAOs)

recommendations: Consider splitting large schema.dart file into separate table definitions for maintainability
Add validation constraints to prevent invalid data states
Implement automated soft delete cleanup in sync process
Add migration strategy for schema evolution
Consider adding database version constants for migration tracking
Implement JSON serialization/deserialization utilities for complex fields
Add comprehensive unit tests for database schema constraints and relationships
